zh.Walgrave Holtolke, Northamton, England: 英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾瓦爾格拉福

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Neville於1290年11月28日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾瓦爾格拉福時，她的父親James de Neville-10歲，母親Mrs James de Neville30歲。她於大約1302年與Walter Waldegrave在英國諾薩普頓史爾瓦爾格拉福結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1346年11月28日在她的家鄉享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Neville于1290年11月28日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾瓦爾格拉福时，她的父亲James de Neville-10岁，母亲Mrs James de Neville30岁。她于大约1302年与Walter Waldegrave在英国諾薩普頓史爾瓦爾格拉福结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1346年11月28日在她的家乡享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

variants.name: Nevells
meanings: Irish: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevils
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Novel
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .
Catalan: variant of   Novell  .

variants.name: Newell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .
English: variant of   Noel  .
English: variant of   Newhall  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Neville was born on 28 November 1290, in Walgrave, Northamptonshire, England, her father, James de Neville, was -10 and her mother, Mrs James de Neville, was 30. She married Walter Waldegrave about 1302, in Walgrave, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 28 November 1346, in her hometown, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Neville est née le 28 novembre 1290, à Walgrave, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, James de Neville, avait -10 ans et sa mère, Mrs James de Neville, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Walter Waldegrave environ 1302, à Walgrave, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 novembre 1346, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Neville am 28. November 1290 in Walgrave, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James de Neville -10 und ihre Mutter Mrs James de Neville 30. Sie hat um 1302 in Walgrave, Northamptonshire, England Walter Waldegrave geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. November 1346 im Alter von 56 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Neville è nata il il 28 novembre 1290 nel Walgrave, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, James de Neville, aveva -10 anni e sua madre, Mrs James de Neville, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Walter Waldegrave circa 1302, nel Walgrave, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 28 novembre 1346, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Nevilleが1290年11月28日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアワルグラブで生まれた時，父James de Nevilleは-10歳，母Mrs James de Nevilleは30歳であった。約1302年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアワルグラブでWalter Waldegraveと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1346年11月28日に故郷で56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Neville(이)가 1290년 11월 28일 영국 노르섬프토실르 월그레이브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James de Neville(은)는 -10세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs James de Neville(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1302년 영국 노르섬프토실르 월그레이브에서 Walter Waldegrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1346년 11월 28일 그녀의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Neville nasceu em 28 de novembro de 1290, em Walgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, James de Neville, tinha -10 anos e sua mãe, Mrs James de Neville, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Walter Waldegrave aproximadamente 1302, em Walgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de novembro de 1346, em sua cidade natal, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Neville родилась 28 ноября 1290 г. в местности валграв, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени James de Neville было -10 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs James de Neville, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Waldegrave около 1302 г. в местности валграв, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 28 ноября 1346 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 56 лет.

Se casó con Walter Waldegrave aproximadamente 1302, en Walgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 28 de noviembre de 1346, en su pueblo, a la edad de 56 años.
